---
Key Market Insights
- Telecom sector strength appears to have lifted both Verizon (VZ) and AT&T (T) today. Keep an eye on broader sector trends as this could be a continuing tailwind.
- The market is carefully watching economic data and central bank actions, so expect volatility around major announcements. This is especially relevant for financial stocks like JPMorgan Chase (JPM).
- Overall U.S. market performance was mixed as investors are anticipating developments from upcoming U.S.-China trade talks. This uncertainty impacts broad market indexes like the SPY and QQQ.

News That Mattered Today
- "Telecom and energy sectors help lift S&P/TSX composite, U.S. markets mixed" — The telecom and energy sectors were strong, helping lift the S&P/TSX composite. Meanwhile, U.S. markets were mixed as investors awaited a U.S.-China meeting on trade.
- "Treasury Secretary Criticizes Senate For Failing To Advance Stablecoin Bill" — The Treasury Secretary criticized the Senate's failure to advance a stablecoin bill, which aimed to establish a federal regulatory framework. This leaves the stablecoin market in a regulatory gray area.
- "Founder of Crypto Platform Celsius Sentenced to 12 Years in Jail for Fraud" — Alexander Mashinsky, founder of Celsius Network, was sentenced to 12 years in prison for fraud after misleading customers about the company's crypto token CEL.
